---
title: "model_fitting"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-28"
---
#now we fit the 2 models (WBA and SBA) to each dataset & then compare which model "best explains" which dataset (3 datasets: selfish, balanced, social)

#pareto k are high for SBA

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, # Data manipulation and plotting
  dplyr,
  here, # Robust file paths
  ggplot2, #ggplot
  cmdstanr,
  loo,
  bayesplot #Plotting MCMC
)

# Parallel Setup: Leave 2 cores free for system stability
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 2

# Optional: Set a default ggplot theme
theme_set(theme_classic())
```

#note: SBA is just a WBA with specific parameters (rho and kappa), but it does exist as a separate model as well

```{r}
#to force it to overwrite (regen) simulations
regenerate_simulations_SBA <-  FALSE 
regenerate_simulations_WBA <-  FALSE

#1. SETTING THE STAN DATA
make_stan_data <- function(df) {
  list(
    n                 = nrow(df),
    trust1            = as.integer(df$FirstRating - 1L), #just to fix it to the right space (its adjusted for plots in the simulating data, 1-8 originally) --> recheck if this works ok (it should be fine?)
    trust2            = as.integer(df$SecondRating - 1L),
    group_trust_mean = as.integer(round(df$GroupTrustMean)), #this should be correct and not need -1L (recheck later though)
    n_total_rating    = 7L,
    prior_kappa_mu    = 2.0,
    prior_kappa_sigma = 1.0
  ) #(alpha and beta priors are hardcoded 0.5 in the models: jeffers priors)
}

datasets <- list(
  self_focused        = read_csv(("data/self_focused_WBA_agent.csv"), n_max=25000), #ONLY take first 25k out of 80k (maybe still too much)
  balanced            = read_csv(("data/SBA_agent.csv"), n_max=25000),
  socially_influenced = read_csv(("data/socially_influenced_WBA_agent.csv"), n_max=25000)
)

sba_model <- cmdstan_model(("models/SBA_model2.stan"))
wba_model <- cmdstan_model(("models/WBA_model2.stan"))

dir.create(("models/fits"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Save only the minimum needed for diagnostics (i was saving full model fits...)
save_fit_slim <- function(fit, file_path, fixed_param = FALSE) {
  log_lik_matrix <- fit$draws("log_lik", format = "matrix") #think this slows down saving considerably
  slim <- list(
    diagnostic_summary = if (fixed_param) NULL else fit$diagnostic_summary(), # for divergences & E-BFMI
    summary            = if (fixed_param) NULL else fit$summary(),             # for rhat, ess_bulk, ess_tail
    loo                = loo(fit$draws("log_lik", format = "matrix"))                # for LOO (available for both)
  )
  saveRDS(slim, file_path)
  rm(log_lik_matrix)
  gc()
}

#2. FITTING MODELS
results <- map(names(datasets), function(dataset_name) {
  
  df <- datasets[[dataset_name]]
  d <- make_stan_data(df)
  d_sba <- d[!names(d) %in% c("prior_kappa_mu", "prior_kappa_sigma")] #doesnt use any of these in the model
  
  # filenames
  sba_file <- file.path("models/fits", paste0(dataset_name, "_sba_fit.rds"))
  wba_file <- file.path("models/fits", paste0(dataset_name, "_wba_fit.rds"))
  
  # SBA (though its effectively a type of WBA)
  if (regenerate_simulations_SBA || !file.exists(sba_file)) {
    sba_fit <- sba_model$sample(
      data          = d_sba, #to omit the data it doesnt need
      fixed_param   = TRUE, #no freefloating params (so no warmup either)
      chains        = 2,
      parallel_chains = min(2, n_cores),  #low to save time; kinda redundant to use min
      iter_sampling = 1000,
      refresh       = 100,
      seed = 123
      )
    t1 <- Sys.time()
    save_fit_slim(sba_fit, sba_file, fixed_param = TRUE) # slim save: diagnostic_summary + summary + log_lik only
    cat("SBA fit saved to:", sba_file, "\n")
    cat("Save time:", difftime(Sys.time(), t1, units = "secs"), "s\n")
    
    #to avoid clogging RAM (it adds up)
    rm(sba_fit)
    gc()
    
  } else {
    cat("Existing SBA fit found:", sba_file, "\n")
  }
  
  # WBA
  if (regenerate_simulations_WBA || !file.exists(wba_file)) {
    wba_fit <- wba_model$sample(
      data            = d,
      chains          = 4, #should be an ok amount of chains 
      parallel_chains = min(4, n_cores), #kinda redundant using min
      iter_warmup     = 500, #warmups are slightly lower than 50/50 but still takes forever...
      iter_sampling   = 1000, #this takes a while, should have done less (could be a model issue?)
      refresh         = 100,
      seed = 123
      )
    t1 <- Sys.time()
    save_fit_slim(wba_fit, wba_file) # slim save: diagnostic_summary + summary + log_lik only
    cat("WBA fit saved to:", wba_file, "\n")
    cat("Save time:", difftime(Sys.time(), t1, units = "secs"), "s\n")
    
    #to avoid clogging RAM (it adds up)
    rm(wba_fit)
    gc()
    
  } else {
    cat("Existing WBA fit found:", wba_file, "\n")
  }
  
  #just save filepaths for later to avoid keeping them in mem
  list(
    sba_file = sba_file,
    wba_file = wba_file
  )
  
})
names(results) <- names(datasets)
saveRDS(results, ("models/fits/results_paths.rds")) #this is specific to my path though
```
